document.title: AKTI ERP Phase 2 Autonomous Branch Trial Plan
document.purpose: Define the controlled Phase 2 autonomous execution run for Codex. This document is a strategy/control plan, not implementation approval.
document.principle: ChatGPT designs and audits. Codex validates and executes. The coordinator transfers outputs between them. Codex executes only the approved ticket queue.

branch_run.name: AKTI ERP Phase 2 Autonomous Execution Run
branch_run.goal: Validate whether Codex can execute a full approved Phase 2 ticket queue autonomously for an extended run while preserving scope, architecture, validation, and auditability.
branch_run.important_note: This is a disposable operational branch. Passing the controlled branch run does not automatically make the branch production-ready or merge-ready.
branch_run.main_branch_policy: main is protected; Codex must never merge to main automatically.
branch_run.branch_base: current merged main containing Phase 1 final implementation plus Phase 2 control documents and approved control-document corrections
branch_run.final_phase_1_implementation_commit: 18f497d P1H-FINAL harden access reads and capability duplicates
branch_run.branch_creation_rule: Create phase2/autonomous-full-run only from clean current main after control-document corrections are merged.
branch_run.v3_autonomy_summary: V3 allows Codex to continue ticket-to-ticket through complete Phase 2 when decision rules pass, while preserving hard gates and final gate stops.

queue_status_policy.AUTONOMOUS_READY: May run next after normal prechecks.
queue_status_policy.AUTONOMOUS_AFTER_PREVIOUS: May run after prior ticket passes validation and artifacts.
queue_status_policy.AUTONOMOUS_DECISION_RULE: May continue only by applying the explicit autonomous_decision_rule for the ticket.
queue_status_policy.GOVERNANCE_BLOCKED: Reserved status for unresolved governance constraints; Phase 2C is currently unlocked under the ADR-0003 formal exception.

v3_queue.title: Create Engagement Gateway Lite contracts and manifest boundary
Implement Engagement Gateway Lite API/service foundation
Lead Desk contract and capability boundary
Lead Desk schema and registry foundation
Lead Desk create and assignment frontend

v3_hard_gates: Required file outside exact ticket scope
Secret access, inspection, printing, copying, exporting, or use required
Active control docs need mutation outside an explicit control-doc correction ticket
Invented business rule, capability, permission, event, module, role, or screen required
Migration scaffolding required but not explicitly ticketed
Fake dashboards or fake operational data
Ticket artifacts cannot be created or verified
Dirty branch at ticket start except ignored review artifacts
branch_run.bootstrap_metadata_note: next_executable_ticket is bootstrap/reference metadata and is not a live per-ticket execution ledger.

included.name: Engagement Gateway Lite
included.purpose: Create the messaging/engagement transport abstraction before Lead Desk depends on messaging.

included.name: Lead Desk Core
included.purpose: Create the lead-management/admissions workflow without direct WhatsApp/Meta coupling.

included.name: WhatsApp Lead Desk Integration
included.purpose: Connect Lead Desk to WhatsApp only through Engagement Gateway Lite or future Engagement Hub boundary.

architecture_boundary: Lead Desk must not call Meta WhatsApp API directly.
All messaging transport must go through Engagement Gateway Lite or future Engagement Hub.
Temporary CRM/shared inbox remains operationally separate until Phase 2C is tested and accepted.

not_allowed: Codex inventing missing tickets or business rules
Direct WhatsApp/Meta API calls from Lead Desk
Frontend screens without approved screen contracts
Unapproved modules such as LMS, HR, Finance, Hiring, Certification, Website Builder, AI Content Editor
Stopping after each successful ticket only because control-doc progress fields are stale

optional_later.decision: Optional read-only pilot later, not mandatory for first run.
Allowed only for frontend tickets after screen contracts exist.
Recommended read-only context tool if available.

required_preconditions: The execution pack exists in docs/process/
Codex has validated these documents against repo reality in Plan Mode
Corrections, if any, are applied before /goal starts; control docs remain stable run contract during execution

The operational branch starts from current clean main after Phase 2 control-document corrections are merged; final Phase 1 implementation commit is 18f497d and Phase 2 control-doc merge commit is f02ac95
Working tree is clean before branch creation
No autonomous run starts without an approved active execution pack
P2-VAL-001 has provided a Phase 2-aware registry verification path before schema-changing tickets run
P2-VAL-002 has provided Phase 2 screen-contract validation support before Lead Desk screen contracts are added
The Codex execution environment is approved safe mode: not danger-full-access, not unrestricted network, not approval-never, and no production secrets
P2-000 is complete and P2A-001 is complete at commit 944d0c84badbf9633c12c395f8e50cb08cc96571
The active execution pack has adopted the v3 queue and next executable ticket is P2A-002
Phase 2C is unlocked under ADR-0003 formal exception and remains limited to stub/non-production implementation constraints

codex_config_recommendation.network_access: disabled unless explicitly approved for a ticket

multi_agent_policy.edit_policy: Main worker edits files. Subagents are review-only unless a ticket explicitly delegates non-overlapping files.

technical: Approved ticket queue executed in order
Per-ticket commits exist on operational branch
P2-VAL-001 and P2-VAL-002 complete before dependent schema or screen-contract tickets
V3 autonomous decision rules are followed before continuing through decision-rule tickets
P2A-001 artifact backfill is acknowledged and future ticket artifacts are created before commit
Per-ticket lightweight artifacts exist (<ticket-id>-summary.md and <ticket-id>-changed-files.zip), with heavy audits at phase gates/final only
architecture: No direct Lead Desk to WhatsApp/Meta API calls
No invented capabilities, roles, permissions, events, or screens
No frontend screen without screen contract
No schema/registry drift outside approved tickets
Access Core and Gatekeeper boundaries preserved
failure_success: If Codex fails, it stops cleanly
Failure point is identifiable by ticket
Lessons identify the safe future batch size

coordinator_workflow: Place these documents under docs/process/ and PLANS.md at repo root.
Ask Codex to validate them in Plan Mode only.
Bring Codex validation result back to ChatGPT.
After PASS, Codex may start the Phase 2 autonomous execution run on the operational branch.
When Codex stops or completes, bring final artifacts back to ChatGPT for audit.
codex_plan_mode_validation_prompt:
We are in Plan Mode only.

Validate the Phase 2 autonomous control documents against the current repo.

Do not implement.
Do not create branches.
Do not run /goal.
Do not modify files.
Do not commit.
Do not push.

Inspect:
- AGENTS.md
- docs/adr/*
- docs/specs/*
- docs/process/*
- packages/contracts/*
- prisma/schema.prisma
- generated/entity-registry.generated.json
- apps/api/*
- apps/web/*
- package.json
- pnpm-workspace.yaml
- CI workflows

Return only:
1. Files inspected
2. Whether these control documents match repo reality
3. Missing files/paths/scripts
4. Unsafe assumptions
5. Corrections required
6. Whether the documents are safe to use as the active autonomous run contract

Do not write code.

phase2c_exception_constraints: Phase 2C is unlocked only for stub/non-production implementation under ADR-0003 formal exception (2026-05-24).
No production credentials for WhatsApp/Meta are allowed in Phase 2C.
No real outbound WhatsApp messages are allowed in Phase 2C.
No direct Lead Desk to Meta/WhatsApp coupling is allowed; transport must pass through Engagement Gateway Lite.
Exception does not authorize Phase 3 or non-Phase-2 scope.
execution_controls.p2b_gate_continuation_policy: P2B-GATE is a validation checkpoint. If validation passes, continue automatically into P2C. If validation fails, stop.
execution_controls.full_phase2_autonomy_statement: Codex may run complete Phase 2 autonomously from P2-VAL-002 through P2C-GATE and stops only on hard gate, validation failure twice, RUN_STATE_CONFLICT, or final run completion.

allowed_when_all_true: failure is deterministic from schema/type/test/lint output
fix is limited to the active ticket allowed files
no architecture/control-doc/ADR change is required, except explicit control-doc correction ticket
no business rule, role, permission, capability, module, event, status, workflow, or screen must be invented
auto_repairable_examples: TypeScript type mismatch inside active ticket files
missing required capability key when an approved capability already exists
local test expectation mismatch caused by active ticket implementation
non_repairable_hard_stops: Prisma/schema change not allowed by ticket
fake data or hardcoded tenant/campus/role/user/org assumptions

allowed_when_all_true: tests exist or are being added by active ticket
existing package script excludes active-ticket tests
wiring change only includes active-ticket tests
no unrelated script behavior is changed
allowed_files_by_context: root package.json only when explicit root orchestration is required
validation_test_runner_wiring_policy.documentation_requirement: Any validation wiring update must be recorded in the ticket summary artifact.
